title: referrals — field classification
description: Referrals data is a mix of commercial reference data (public within tenant admins — program config), operational identifiers (referrer/referred user IDs, code strings), financial records (reward amounts, currency, payment status), fraud evidence (hashed device fingerprints, IP hashes, disposable-email flags), and marketing-adjacent identifiers (click IDs, UTM parameters in the attribution snapshot). No health / medical / minor-specific data on any row — those attach through the User side.

confidential: ReferralProgram.reward_amount_referrer (tenant-competitive — competitors would benefit from seeing reward tables)
ReferralProgram.fraud_config (fraud thresholds — leaking to attackers helps them evade detection)
Referral.attribution_snapshot (composite — contains click IDs + UTM + user IDs that could enable cross-tenant inference)
ReferralFraudFlag.details (fraud evidence — competitor tenants gaining access would learn detection heuristics)
restricted: Referral.device_snapshot (hashed but privacy-restricted — combines UA + IP + fingerprint hashes)
audit_log: ReferralProgram.reward_type_* + reward_amount_* (frozen after first referral commits)

notes: Referred_email is the highest-risk PII in the module — captured pre-signup, retained 7 years, redacted at 90 days post user-erasure. The Redactor pipeline strips it on egress for callers who lack referrals.view-pii permission (a follow-up permission for the compliance surface).

attribution_snapshot is a COMPOSITE — it carries UTM + click IDs (business-sensitive) + user IDs (PII) + device data (hashed). Egress rendering routes through AttributionSnapshotBuilder's redactor to strip user IDs when the caller lacks permission.
device_snapshot stores ONLY hashes (SHA-256 of UA + IP + fingerprint) — never raw values. The RedactedFingerprint contract prohibits raw storage.
regulated_financial fields align with 7-year retention. Extended to 10y with Enterprise `referrals_extended_retention`.
No PCI-DSS scope: NO card data / PAN / CVV on any owned row. Financial linkage flows through the Finance module (Wave 4) via finance_credit_id.
fraud_config on the program row is CONFIDENTIAL because leaking the thresholds to attackers lets them evade detection. Only admin/owner can read it via the API.
